Creator: paul.martin@collegemoneymethod.com
Description:
Hi,

Thank you for scheduling time to meet with me. Can you share your 2-3 main goals for our meeting? What would you like to get out of the hour?

Please feel free to share any background information that will help me prepare for our advising session. Our meeting is confidential.

If you'd like to determine your eligibility for need-based financial aid, please have your taxes and other financial information during our session. Here is information that would make our time most productive.

  1. 2024 tax returns
  2. Overall balances for your investments outside of retirement, including:
    1. Savings, checking
    2. Brokerage
    3. 529 Plans or other college savings plans
    4. Trusts
  3. If you own your home, information about your primary home, including:
    1. Purchase price
    2. Current market value
    3. Outside mortgage
  4. If you own additional properties, the equity value of those properties
  5. Overall details on personal business
  6. Medical expense estimates

I look forward to meeting you and providing as much guidance as possible during our one-hour session.

Kind regards,

Paul

You'll meet on a web conference https://appt.link/m/2GbNjgfgyi/conference Student -------------------- Amy Salama Grade -------------------- 12th School -------------------- Marin Academy Please share the 2-3 goals you have for our advising session. -------------------- Amy has been accepted to UPenn and has an excellent, non-loan financial aid offer of $75,406 for the 2026-2027 school year (I have forwarded the offer to you by email).

As you might remember from when we talked about Amy's sibling Skye's financial aid, I have had very high insured medical costs for Skye's mental health treatment, I have very high debt service costs from the loans I took to cover that treatment when Skye and Amy's dad wouldn't pay any child support, and my salary has been $225k (we are in the SF Bay Area, so that's not much). I just started a new job last month that pays $250k.

My main goals for our call are:

(1) get your confirmation that this is the best we are likely to get for 2026-27 (the $21k we'll need to pay is still a challenge); and

(2) strategize re anything we can do now to try to keep this great award during the time Amy is at Penn - I am kind of terrified it will go up and we won't be able to afford it.

Thank you!

Description: Navigating financial aid can be a confusing process for refugees, immigrants, mixed-status families, and other forcibly displaced youth. While their experiences may overlap in some ways with unaccompanied homeless youth, there are distinct differences. 

In this webinar, participants will hear from the Presidents' Alliance on Higher Education, gain an understanding of how various immigration statuses affect access to financial aid, and explore both the similarities and distinctions compared to the experience of unaccompanied homeless youth.

Description:
I'm inviting you to join this AI Workflow Systems for B2B Operational Growth workshop as we both understand the importance of building scalable systems. The landscape of B2B SaaS and consulting is rapidly evolving, and staying ahead requires embracing the latest AI and automation frameworks.

This session is specifically designed for CEOs, Founders, and RevOps Leaders who are ready to build consistent, scalable funnels for predictable growth.

Our proven funnels and automation builds have helped over 200 organizations achieve remarkable results. This workshop is our way of sharing these proven strategies with forward-thinking professionals like you.

Over the years, we've generated substantial returns for clients and developed effective approaches that enable them to succeed. This workshop will reveal our comprehensive framework for scaling RevOps the right way that can:

✅ Build consistently working lead generation funnels that scale beyond referrals.

✅ Address the common challenge of outsourcing lead generation before proving a working funnel.

✅ Implement a systematic A/B testing process to methodically improve funnel effectiveness.

✅ Streamline operations by automating core engines for outreach, content, and lead nurturing.

✅ Gain full-funnel visibility with integrated reporting to diagnose issues and run effective tests.

In this workshop, we'll deep dive into 2 key solutions:

  1. Omni-channel Content, Outreach & Lead Nurturing Engines: A comprehensive automation system that handles content creation across multiple formats, manages prospecting and personalized outreach across Email, LinkedIn, and SMS, while automatically nurturing leads through intelligent segmentation and follow-up sequences to maximize conversion at every stage.

  2. A Growth Experimentation Framework: to methodically grow your prospect, outreach, content and funnel lead volumes step by step, predictably.

We will conclude with a live Q&A to address your specific questions and implementation needs.

Notifications (please select):

Yes or Maybe - You'll receive the direct session link and confirmation.

No - We respect your time; you will not receive further communications about this event.

About Us: We are a team of engineers, automation specialists, and marketers focused on building scalable revenue systems. Over the past six years, our team has helped clients achieve significant growth through AI-focused consulting, working with over 200 organizations to help them build consistent, scalable funnels.

Join us for this exclusive workshop where we'll share proven funnels, real case studies, and live automation builds. This session is specifically designed for leaders in SaaS, consulting, and coaching businesses. See you there!
Title: AI-Powered Workflow Systems to 3-7X Your B2B Revenue Operations

Description:
Topic: Counseling Students Through OB3 Loan Changes
Description: Federal student loan rules are changing in meaningful ways beginning July 1, 2026, with important implications for students, families, and the counselors who guide them. In partnership with ACCIS, this Juno-led webinar will provide a clear, counselor-focused overview of upcoming federal loan changes affecting undergraduate families, graduate and professional students, and parents.

The session will focus on how these changes intersect with common counseling conversations: enrollment decisions, FAFSA, funding gaps, transfers, and leaves of absence, while respecting the professional boundaries counselors navigate every day. We will emphasize awareness, risk identification, and counselor-appropriate framing rather than prescriptive financial advice.

Designed for high school counselors, this webinar will also highlight equity considerations and common questions families are already asking. Our goal is to help counselors feel informed, prepared, and confident as these new rules take effect.

We look forward to learning together and supporting the work you do with students and families.

Description:
Hi,

Thank you for scheduling time to meet with me. Can you share your 2-3 main goals for our meeting? What would you like to get out of the hour?

Please feel free to share any background information that will help me prepare for our advising session.

If you'd like to determine your eligibility for need-based financial aid, please have your taxes and other financial information during our session. Here is information that would make our time most productive.

  1. 2024 tax returns and W-2s
  2. Overall balances for your investments outside of retirement, including:
    1. Savings, checking
    2. Brokerage
    3. 529 Plans or other college savings plans
    4. Trusts
  3. If you own your home, information about your primary home, including:
    1. Purchase price
    2. Current market value
    3. Outside mortgage
  4. If you own additional properties, the equity value of those properties
  5. Overall details on personal business
  6. Medical expense estimates

I look forward to meeting you and providing as much guidance as possible during our one-hour session.

If you need to reschedule, you will need to do so at least 24 hours before our meeting.

Kind regards,

Paul

Creator: paul.martin@collegemoneymethod.com
Description:
Hi,

Thank you for scheduling time to meet with me. Can you share your 2-3 main goals for our meeting? What would you like to get out of the hour?

Please feel free to share any background information that will help me prepare for our advising session.

If you'd like to determine your eligibility for need-based financial aid, please have your taxes and other financial information during our session. Here is information that would make our time most productive.

  1. 2024 tax returns and W-2s
  2. Overall balances for your investments outside of retirement, including:
    1. Savings, checking
    2. Brokerage
    3. 529 Plans or other college savings plans
    4. Trusts
  3. If you own your home, information about your primary home, including:
    1. Purchase price
    2. Current market value
    3. Outside mortgage
  4. If you own additional properties, the equity value of those properties
  5. Overall details on personal business
  6. Medical expense estimates

I look forward to meeting you and providing as much guidance as possible during our one-hour session.

If you need to reschedule, you will need to do so at least 24 hours before our meeting.

Kind regards,

Paul

paul.martin@collegemoneymethod.com

Topic: All In and Ready Webinar Series
Description: CSAC is driven by its mission to promote educational equity by making postsecondary education affordable for all Californians. CSAC is proud to host monthly webinars to share expertise and best practices on financial aid applications completion and meet the goals of the CSAC Student Success Blueprint. 

These webinars provide a platform to convene as a community, to: 
•	Hear the latest federal and state updates on financial aid. 
•	Learn about CSAC’s tools and resources. 
•	Listen to experts and partners in the field. 
•	Address state emergencies and urgent access and success barriers. 

Monthly webinars are held every second Thursday of the month from 9:00 - 10:00 a.m. This is a recurring webinar registration. Starting September 2025, attendees register once and can attend all the occurrences.

Title: Financial Aid Advising for Brooklyn Friends School Families -- Cheryl Nicholas

Creator: paul.martin@collegemoneymethod.com
Description:
Hi,

Thank you for scheduling time to meet with me. Can you share your 2-3 main goals for our meeting? What would you like to get out of the hour?

Please feel free to share any background information that will help me prepare for our advising session. Our meeting is confidential.

If you'd like to determine your eligibility for need-based financial aid, please have your taxes and other financial information during our session. Here is information that would make our time most productive.

  1. 2024 tax returns
  2. Overall balances for your investments outside of retirement, including:
    1. Savings, checking
    2. Brokerage
    3. 529 Plans or other college savings plans
    4. Trusts
  3. If you own your home, information about your primary home, including:
    1. Purchase price
    2. Current market value
    3. Outside mortgage
  4. If you own additional properties, the equity value of those properties
  5. Overall details on personal business
  6. Medical expense estimates

I look forward to meeting you and providing as much guidance as possible during our one-hour session.

Kind regards,

Paul

Description: CSAC is driven by its mission to promote educational equity by making postsecondary education affordable for all Californians. CSAC is proud to host monthly webinars to share expertise and best practices on financial aid applications completion and meet the goals of the CSAC Student Success Blueprint. 

These webinars provide a platform to convene as a community, to: 
•	Hear the latest federal and state updates on financial aid. 
•	Learn about CSAC’s tools and resources. 
•	Listen to experts and partners in the field. 
•	Address state emergencies and urgent access and success barriers. 

Monthly webinars are held every second Thursday of the month from 9:00 - 10:00 a.m. This is a recurring webinar registration. Starting September 2025, attendees register once and can attend all the occurrences.

Description
This workshop and resources guide you through the senior-year process to complete timely and accurate financial aid applications and help advocate for your unique circumstances. It includes how to find, track and apply for merit aid from colleges.

Here are insights and actions you'll take away from this session:

● How colleges determine your financial aid eligibility
● Steps to prepare for the financial aid applications.
● Timelines and deadlines for the FAFSA and CSS Profile.
● Which parents participate and submit applications.
● Key differences between the FAFSA and CSS Profile.
● How to report financial information on each application.
● How to find and apply for merit-based scholarships at your colleges.

Title: Financial Aid Advising for Kent Denver Families -- Shreka Thomas

Creator: paul.martin@collegemoneymethod.com
Description:
Hi,

Thank you for scheduling time to meet with me. Can you share your 2-3 main goals for our meeting? What would you like to get out of the hour?

Please feel free to share any background information that will help me prepare for our advising session.

If you'd like to determine your eligibility for need-based financial aid, please have your taxes and other financial information during our session. Here is information that would make our time most productive.

  1. 2024 tax returns
  2. Overall balances for your investments outside of retirement, including:
    1. Savings, checking
    2. Brokerage
    3. 529 Plans or other college savings plans
    4. Trusts
  3. If you own your home, information about your primary home, including:
    1. Purchase price
    2. Current market value
    3. Outside mortgage
  4. If you own additional properties, the equity value of those properties
  5. Overall details on personal business
  6. Medical expense estimates

I look forward to meeting you and providing as much guidance as possible during our one-hour session.

Please understand, if you need to reschedule, you need to do so at least 24-hours notice prior to our meeting.

Kind regards,

Paul

Topic: All In and Ready Webinar Series
Description: CSAC is driven by its mission to promote educational equity by making postsecondary education affordable for all Californians. CSAC is proud to host monthly webinars to share expertise and best practices on financial aid applications completion and meet the goals of the CSAC Student Success Blueprint. 

These webinars provide a platform to convene as a community, to: 
•	Hear the latest federal and state updates on financial aid. 
•	Learn about CSAC’s tools and resources. 
•	Listen to experts and partners in the field. 
•	Address state emergencies and urgent access and success barriers. 

Monthly webinars are held every second Thursday of the month from 9:00 - 10:00 a.m. This is a recurring webinar registration. Starting September 2025, attendees register once and can attend all the occurrences.

Creator: paul.martin@collegemoneymethod.com
Description:
Hi,

Thank you for scheduling time to meet with me. Can you share your 2-3 main goals for our meeting? What would you like to get out of the hour?

Please feel free to share any background information that will help me prepare for our advising session. Our meeting is confidential.

If you'd like to determine your eligibility for need-based financial aid, please have your taxes and other financial information during our session. Here is information that would make our time most productive.

  1. 2023 tax returns
  2. Overall balances for your investments outside of retirement, including:
    1. Savings, checking
    2. Brokerage
    3. 529 Plans or other college savings plans
    4. Trusts
  3. If you own your home, information about your primary home, including:
    1. Purchase price
    2. Current market value
    3. Outside mortgage
  4. If you own additional properties, the equity value of those properties
  5. Overall details on personal business
  6. Medical expense estimates

I look forward to meeting you and providing as much guidance as possible during our one-hour session.

Kind regards,

Paul

CSAC is proud to host monthly webinars to share expertise and best practices on financial aid applications completion and meet the goals of the CSAC Student Success Blueprint. 

These webinars provide a platform to convene as a community, to:

•	Hear the latest federal and state updates on financial aid. 
•	Learn about CSAC’s tools and resources. 
•	Listen to experts and partners in the field.
